input.prompt:
I have a .NET class library at src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj that I want to publish
to nuget.org using trusted publishing (OIDC) instead of an API key.
The repo is hosted on GitHub at myorg/mylib. Help me set this up.

data.content:
The directory is empty, so I'll scaffold everything from scratch. Here's the plan:

1. **`src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj`** — with NuGet package metadata
2. **`.github/workflows/publish.yml`** — GitHub Actions workflow using OIDC trusted publishing
3. **NuGet.org setup instructions** — you'll need to do a one-time config on nuget.org

Let me create the files.
data.reasoningText:
The directory is empty. I need to create the project structure and the GitHub Actions workflow for trusted publishing. Let me set up:

1. The .csproj file with NuGet package metadata
2. A GitHub Actions workflow that uses OIDC trusted publishing

Let me create the necessary files.

arguments.file_text:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>

    <!-- NuGet package metadata -->
    <PackageId>MyLib</PackageId>
    <Version>1.0.0</Version>
    <Authors>myorg</Authors>
    <Description>MyLib class library</Description>
    <PackageProjectUrl>https://github.com/myorg/mylib</PackageProjectUrl>
    <RepositoryUrl>https://github.com/myorg/mylib</RepositoryUrl>
    <RepositoryType>git</RepositoryType>
    <PackageLicenseExpression>MIT</PackageLicenseExpression>
    <PackageReadmeFile>README.md</PackageReadmeFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Include repo-root README in the package -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <None Include="../../README.md" Pack="true" PackagePath="/" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>

name: Publish to NuGet

on:
  release:
    types: [published]

jobs:
  publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    permissions:
      # Required for OIDC trusted publishing
      id-token: write
      contents: read

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
        with:
          dotnet-version: '8.0.x'

      - name: Pack
        run: >
          dotnet pack src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj
          --configuration Release
          -p:Version=${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}
          --output ./artifacts

      - name: Publish to NuGet (trusted publishing / OIDC)
        run: >
          dotnet nuget push ./artifacts/*.nupkg
          --source https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json

data.content:
Done. Here's what was created and what you still need to do:

---

### Files created

- **`src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj`** — includes NuGet package metadata (update `PackageId`, `Description`, etc. to match your library).
- **`.github/workflows/publish.yml`** — triggers on GitHub Release, packs the project, and pushes via `dotnet nuget push` **with no API key** (OIDC handles auth).

### Key workflow details

- **`permissions: id-token: write`** — lets the runner request an OIDC token.
- **No `--api-key`** — NuGet.org exchanges the GitHub OIDC token for authorization automatically.
- **Version** is pulled from the release tag name.

---

### One-time setup on nuget.org

1. Go to **nuget.org → Manage Packages → [your package] → Trusted Publishers** (or set it up during initial package creation).
2. Add a **GitHub Actions** trusted publisher with:
   | Field | Value |
   |---|---|
   | Repository owner | `myorg` |
   | Repository name | `mylib` |
   | Workflow | `publish.yml` |
   | Environment | *(leave blank)* |

This tells NuGet.org to accept OIDC tokens from that specific workflow.

### To publish

Create a GitHub Release with a semver tag (e.g., `1.0.0`), and the workflow will automatically pack and push.
